articles.title: Heatwave Alert in Telangana and AP: 44 Zones Under Severe Heat, Winds Gusting Up to 40 kmph - Munsif News 24x7
articles.full_text:
Hyderabad/Amaravati: A strong heatwave in Andhra Pradesh and Telangana, accompanied by winds gusting up to 40 km per hour, is creating difficult weather conditions across both states. The Meteorological Department has issued warnings of severe heatwave in 44 zones and normal heatwave in 29 zones, while also cautioning about possible thunderstorms and scattered rains.
Table of Contents
Severe Heatwave in Andhra Pradesh: Districts on High Alert
The heatwave in Andhra Pradesh is expected to intensify in several districts:
- Srikakulam, Vizianagaram, and Parvathipuram Manyam districts likely to face high temperatures
- Anakapalli and Parvathipuram Manyam districts may experience severe heatwave conditions
- Several mandals in Srikakulam and Parvathipuram Manyam are expected to be worst affected
On Monday, Parvathipuram recorded the highest temperature of 44.2°C, indicating rising heat levels across the region.
Strong Winds and Thunderstorm Warning in Telangana and AP
Along with extreme heat, winds reaching up to 40 kmph are causing disruption in parts of Telangana.
Weather officials have also warned of:
- Heavy rains with thunderstorms in Manyam and Polavaram districts
- Scattered showers in Kurnool, Nandyal, and Anantapur districts
- Possibility of lightning and gusty winds during rainfall
These sudden weather changes are linked to a developing system in the region.
Surface Circulation Behind Changing Weather Conditions
Officials from the disaster management agency stated that a surface circulation has formed over South Coastal Andhra Pradesh and adjoining areas, influencing current weather patterns.
This system is contributing to:
Heatwave and Weather Alerts Continue Across AP and Telangana
The heatwave in Andhra Pradesh and Telangana, combined with strong winds and thunderstorm warnings, is expected to continue in the coming days. With 44 zones under severe heatwave alert and temperatures already touching 44.2°C, residents are advised to remain cautious. Authorities continue to monitor the situation closely as changing weather patterns impact multiple districts.
Follow MunsifNews24x7 for latest updates.
